raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I was the victim of a coordinated fraud scheme by a merchant operating under the names XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX. 

During the incident, a merchant employee took physical possession of my XXXX, accessed the American Express app without my consent, and processed large, undisclosed charges. I did not authorize these transactions. The merchant split a single {$16000.00} invoice into two charges {$7200.00} and {$8900.00} a known tactic to bypass fraud detection systems. 

I immediately returned all merchandise within one hour. The merchant accepted the return, kept all items, and provided a written slip promising a refund that was never issued. The next day, I filed a police report with the XXXX XXXX Police XXXX. 

I reported the fraud to American Express on XX/XX/year>. American Express later issued me a replacement card on XX/XX/XXXX due to confirmed fraud risk. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, the merchant attempted to fabricate a shipment and generate false delivery records after all merchandise had been returned and after the fraud was reported. The merchant asked for my address because they did not have one, and gave shifting explanations of what they were attempting to deliver. 

Despite extensive evidenceincluding a police report, the written refund promise, documentation of unauthorized access, amount manipulation, fabricated shipment attempts, and false statements by the merchantAmerican Express closed my dispute and stated the charges were valid. 

This case meets every criterion for unauthorized use under Regulation Z ( 12 CFR 1026 ). I have repeatedly provided American Express with a complete evidence packet demonstrating clear fraud, and I am requesting that the charges totaling {$16000.00} be refunded. All the evidence and my dispute requests are included in the attached Master Document Packet.
